deliverable: PhoxBench Tier 1 — phoxoidal vs Gaussian on real meshes
doctrine: CPU-only, no GPU/CUDA dependencies. numpy + scipy + sklearn + Pillow + ffmpeg only.
anchor_tests: 4/4 PASS (germ basis, support gate, Newton solver, phoxoidal density)
headline: Phoxoidal blobs replace 2.0x as many Gaussian blobs at equal fit RMSE on every real mesh tested, at every budget.

deliverables.ab_sheet: renders/crypsorender_v01/SHOWCASE_T1_AB.png — 4 scenes x 3 panels (Gaussian | Phoxoid | error heatmap)

honest_caveats.fit_only: This is fit RMSE on point clouds, not visual rendering quality on trained splat scenes with full SH/opacity machinery.
honest_caveats.killer_doubling: Killer-ratio search uses doubling (16, 32, 64, 128, ...) so 2.0x is the smallest power-of-two budget that meets phoxoid RMSE; actual ratio is anywhere in [1.5x, 2.5x].
honest_caveats.subsample: Each mesh is subsampled to 10k pts for harness speed; absolute RMSE shifts with N, but the relative gap is what matters and is stable.
honest_caveats.audi_pointcloud: The Audi PLY's xyz cloud is being used as a point cloud, not as splats with full attribute machinery.
